gateCheck.reason: No critical findings. High/Medium findings are recommendations, not blockers.

findings.description: Uses Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) for timestamp instead of Date object
findings.impact: Mixed timestamp formats - some fields use Date objects, others use Unix seconds. This can cause issues when comparing dates.
findings.recommendation: Use consistent Date objects: updatedAt: new Date()

findings.description: PUT endpoint lacks Zod validation - accepts arbitrary body fields
findings.impact: Invalid data can be submitted, including fields that shouldn't be user-modifiable. No schema validation for updateBusinessSchema.

findings.description: Inconsistent error response format - uses { message: '...' } instead of unified format
findings.impact: Inconsistent API responses make client handling harder

findings.description: N+1 query pattern - separate query for category after business fetch
findings.impact: Each business request triggers 2 additional queries (category + products + reviews). 4 queries per request instead of 1.
findings.recommendation: Use leftJoin to fetch category with business query in single query

findings.impact: Anyone can create blog posts without admin authentication. The comment says 'Admin only' but no auth middleware is applied.
findings.recommendation: Add getCurrentUser() check at start of POST handler, or rely on admin middleware from route mounting

findings.description: Error message string matching for duplicate detection is fragile
findings.impact: Database error messages vary by SQL dialect. 'UNIQUE' check may not catch all duplicate key violations.
findings.recommendation: Use proper database constraint handling or Zod schema validation for slug uniqueness

findings.impact: 404 response doesn't use unified error format with code field

findings.description: getCurrentUser function uses 'any' type for Hono context
findings.impact: Type safety is bypassed - should use proper Hono types
findings.recommendation: Import proper Hono context type or use generic type inference

findings.description: Comment states 'Admin only — protected by admin middleware' but route is standalone
findings.impact: If mounted standalone (not under /admin), this route is NOT protected. Docstring may mislead.
findings.recommendation: Clarify middleware protection assumption in comment

recommendations: 1. Apply updateBusinessSchema validation to PUT /api/businesses/[slug] endpoint
2. Standardize timestamp format - use Date objects consistently throughout
3. Fix N+1 query in [slug].ts by using leftJoin for category
4. Verify blog routes are mounted under admin middleware or add explicit auth
5. Consider using proper typed Hono context instead of 'any'
positiveFindings: Astro sign-in properly uses better-auth's signIn with error handling
Astro create.ts correctly uses api-response helpers and Zod validation
Hono businesses.ts has proper auth patterns with getCurrentUser
Admin routes have rate limiting on login endpoint
Error codes are consistently defined in ErrorCodes enum
SQL injection is mitigated via parameterized queries (Drizzle ORM)
